result.byteIdentityStrategy: Every routing is byte-identical today because cliSessionStore(store,cfg,cityPath) = resolveSessionStore(...) = resolveClassStore(...) returns the input store verbatim (cmd/gc/class_store.go:231-237,269-271), and every new class-typed param receives the SAME concrete store value it received before the split: (1) CLI roots derive sessStore := cliSessionStore(store, cfg, cityPath) from the already-open work store — no second store open; nil cfg (loadCityConfig failure in cmdSessionWait, loadCityConfigWithoutBuiltinPackRefresh failure elsewhere) → identity. (2) All nudge params are beads.NudgesStore{Store: store} wrapping the SAME work store the code wraps today (matching the deliverSlingNudge precedent, cmd_sling.go:1488-1492/1519 — nudge ops stay on the passed work store; do NOT introduce openNudgeBeadStore calls, which would open a second store handle). (3) The controller call-site swaps pass identity-equal values: cr.sessionsBeadStore()/cr.nudgesBeadStore()/cr.cityBeadStore() all collapse to the same city store today (class_store.go doc). (4) Param renames (store→sessStore in loadWaitBeadsForWakeState / loadWaitBeadsForOpenSessions[WithSeen] / clearSessionWaitHoldIfIdle / prepareWaitWakeStateForCityWithSnapshot) are compile-level only. (5) The convenience wrappers prepareWaitWakeState / prepareWaitWakeStateForCity / dispatchReadyWaitNudges keep their signatures and fan the single store into all class params (single-store shape), so ~22 existing test call sites are untouched. result.controllerValidation: Every classification was validated against a controller class-accessor call site, not a static read alone. (1) workerSessionTargetRunningWithConfig / workerKillSessionTargetWithConfig / workerHandleForSessionTargetWithConfig: the session reconciler passes its reconcile store to this exact helper family (session_reconciler.go:259, 1537, 2308, 2904, 2988, 4341), and that store is provably the SESSION-class accessor: city_runtime.go:2137 `sessStore := cr.sessionsBeadStore()` feeds reconcileSessionBeadsTracedWithNamedDemand at city_runtime.go:2294, and `cr.sessionsBeadStore().Store` feeds reconcileSessionBeadsAtPathWithNamedDemand at city_runtime.go:2969-2977. Routed-CLI precedent: cmd_sling.go:1386 and 1838 already pass sessStore to workerSessionTargetRunningWithConfig. => SESSION. (2) clearRestartRequest's sessionFrontDoor(store).ApplyPatch(restart_requested/continuation_reset_pending): the controller constructs its session front door exclusively from the sessions accessor — city_runtime.go:481 `sessionFrontDoor(cr.sessionsBeadStore().Store)` (adoption barrier) and city_runtime.go:2057 (config-drift accept); restart_requested is a session-lifecycle metadata key owned by the reconciler decision path. => SESSION. (3) sessionRestartableByController (resolveSessionID + store.Get + isNamedSessionBead/namedSessionMode) and sessionAgentMetricIdentityByName (resolveSessionID + store.Get + sessionAgentMetricIdentity): the controller sibling sessionAgentMetricIdentity(*session, cfg) at session_reconciler.go:399 reads beads that arrive from the reconciler's session snapshot (loadSessionBeadSnapshot over sessionsBeadStore, city_runtime.go:2147), and named_sessions.go is in snapshotInfoOnlyFiles (session-Info-converted). resolveSessionID is explicitly named in the providers.go relocation route as session-class access (frontdoor_di_guard_test.go:245-254). => SESSION. (4) createHandoffMail / beadmail: the controller builds mail as a TWO-STORE beadmail — newCityMailProvider (class_store.go:288-292) passes msgStore=resolveMailMessagesStore (MESSAGING class) and sessStore=resolveSessionStore (SESSION class) into newMailProviderWithSessionStore; the CLI analog openCityMailProvider (providers.go:845-862) does the identical split with cliSessionStore. beadmail.Provider's own doc (beadmail.go:37-44) states: store persists message beads (messaging class); sessionStore serves addressing/identity session reads. So createHandoffMail's beadmail.New(store) must become beadmail.NewWithStores(store, sessStore): message arm stays MAIL on the passed store, session-addressing arm routes. A sessions-only relocation therefore does NOT drag message writes onto the session store, and mail's session reads follow the relocated session backend — exact controller parity. (5) resolveDefaultMailSenderForCommand's store param: its entire store usage is session-bead access — resolveMailIdentityWithConfigCached -> resolveSessionIDWithConfig (cmd_mail.go:953) + session.NewStore(beads.SessionStore{Store: store}).MailboxAddress (cmd_mail.go:955) + resolveLiveConfiguredNamedMailTargetCached (memoized gc:session enumeration, cmd_mail.go comment 'Memoize the gc:session enumeration') + fallback resolveMailIdentityCached (resolveSessionID + MailboxAddress). Controller parity: this is precisely the 'session reads/writes for addressing/identity resolution' role newCityMailProvider assigns to its sessStore. => SESSION; pass sessStore (no signature change — the helper is class-agnostic; cmd_mail.go's own plain-store call sites are that file's slice). (6) drainOps (GC_DRAIN/GC_DRAIN_ACK/GC_RESTART_REQUESTED/GC_DRIFT_RESTART): pure runtime.Provider SetMeta/GetMeta/RemoveMeta — tmux metadata, zero bead-store access; the reconciler consumes the same dops surface (cr.dops, session_reconciler.go:2368). Nothing to route. (7) The nil-store workerSessionTargetRunningWithConfig calls in doRuntimeDrain/doRuntimeUndrain (cmd_runtime_drain.go:204, 286): controller.go:1032 and 1061 use the identical nil-store form; with store==nil the worker factory takes the pure runtime path — no bead access exists to route.
deferredSites: createHandoffMail message-persistence arm: the `store` first arg (and the msgStore side of beadmail.NewWithStores) stays the plain work store at all three roots. MESSAGING-class routing (resolveMailMessagesStore(store, cfg, cityPath, nil) at cmdHandoff/cmdHandoffRemote, per the openCityMailProvider precedent at providers.go:860) is a separate messaging-class slice — exactly how cmd_sling.go deferred its nudge enqueue to the nudges slice. A sessions-only relocation is unaffected: no session write lands on the mail arm.

cmd_mail.go's own plain-store identity-resolution call sites (resolveDefaultMailSenderForCommandCached at cmd_mail.go:1648, resolveDefaultMailSenderForCommand at cmd_mail.go:2113, and the resolveMailIdentityWithConfigCached family) — cmd_mail.go is not in sessionRelocationRoutedFiles yet; its store routing is that file's own E1 slice. This plan only changes what cmd_handoff.go PASSES to the shared helper; the helper signature is untouched, so no conflict.

cmd_runtime_drain.go's doRuntimeDrain/doRuntimeUndrain/doRuntimeDrainCheck/doRuntimeDrainAck/waitForControllerRestart: nothing deferred — they hold no bead store (drainOps is runtime metadata; the two workerSessionTargetRunningWithConfig calls pass nil store, matching controller.go:1032/1061). Listed here so the red-team knows they were classified, not missed.

result.risks: 1) Deliberate deviation from the literal 'hoist cfg load' instruction in cmdRuntimeRequestRestart: hoisting the existing loadCityConfig(current.cityPath, stderr) above the restartable check would run ensureBuiltinPacksForConfigLoad (disk-writing builtin-pack refresh, cmd_agent.go:40-41) plus stderr warnings on the named-session early-return path (return 0 at line 522) where today NO cfg load ever happens — a real behavior change. The plan instead derives a routing-only cfg via loadCityConfigWithoutBuiltinPackRefresh(current.cityPath, io.Discard) (read-only parse, warnings discarded — the exact openCityMailProvider/cmd_prime hot-path precedent) and leaves line 526's loadCityConfig untouched. Cost: city.toml is parsed twice on the restartable main path. If the red-team prefers a single load, the fallback is hoisting the no-refresh load and REPLACING line 526 with the full loader unchanged in position — do not silently downgrade line 526's loader, its refresh side effect is load-bearing for template resolution in workerHandleForSessionTargetWithConfig. 2) cmdHandoff's auto (PreCompact hook) branch gains a read-only city.toml parse it did not have; byte-identical for beads, negligible latency, but it IS new I/O on a hook path — flag to owner. 3) resolveDefaultMailSenderForCommand is now called with a session-class store from cmd_handoff.go while cmd_mail.go's own call sites still pass the plain store; the helper is class-agnostic so both compile and behave identically today, but the inconsistency persists until the cmd_mail.go slice lands (listed as deferred). 4) The guard is a regression canary, not a completeness proof: the non-front-door session reads (resolveSessionID, store.Get, worker helpers) are protected only by the positive cliSessionStore( tripwire plus the end-to-end relocation acceptance test. 5) doHandoffRemote keeps cfg=nil at the worker calls (lines 411/424) — pre-existing behavior, intentionally NOT 'fixed' here; changing it to pass cfg would alter template resolution and break byte-identity. 6) cmd_runtime_drain.go gains a beads import solely for `var sessStore beads.Store`; goimports must keep it (it is used by the declaration). 7) Verify after implementation: `grep -c 'sessionFrontDoor(store)' cmd/gc/cmd_handoff.go` == 0, `grep -c 'beadmail.New(' cmd/gc/cmd_handoff.go` shows only NewWithStores, and TestSessionRelocationRootsRouteThroughSessionClassStore + TestFrontDoorStoreFreeFilesStayStoreFree pass.
